zh.Upholland, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾武普 霍蘭
zh.lifeSummary: 當Siward Simon de Holland Of Longworth於1146年出生在英國藍卡爾武普 霍蘭時，他的父親Ulf De Holland28歲，母親Eva de Kellett26歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1204年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾武普 霍蘭享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Dutch: genitivized form of   Holland  .

variants.name: Howland
meanings: English: variant of   Holland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Hovland  .

History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollon
meanings: English: altered form of   Holland  .

nameMeanings.name: Siward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sinhard, composed of the elements sin ‘old’ (later ‘sense, opinion’) and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Siniard and Sinyard .

variants.name: Seney
meanings: Altered form of French Senet:
from a pet form of the personal name of ancient Germanic origin Senard, which is a variant of Sinard .
variant of Sené ‘wise, sensible’ (see Sene ). The surname Seney, probably of the same origin, is also found in France (mainly Seine-Maritime). Compare Senay , Senna 4, and Snay 2.

variants.name: Siniard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Andre.
Altered form of French   Sinard  .

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Attainment’.

en.lifeSummary: When Siward Simon de Holland Of Longworth was born in 1146, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, his father, Ulf De Holland, was 28 and his mother, Eva de Kellett, was 26. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1204, in Up Holland, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Siward Simon de Holland Of Longworth est né en 1146, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Ulf De Holland, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Eva de Kellett, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1204, à Up Holland, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Siward Simon de Holland Of Longworth(이)가 1146년 영국 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ulf De Holland(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Eva de Kellett(은)는 26세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1204년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 업 홀랜드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.
